schema.name: TZPID source truth generated from canonical equation master

theory.canonical_statement: \section{Casimir Engineering and Stability} Casimir metamaterials provide the effective stress tensor required to counter gravitational collapse: \begin{equation} P_{\mathrm{Casimir}} = -\frac{\hbar c \pi^2}{240 d^4}, \end{equation} balanced by electromagnetic and quantum pressure: \begin{equation} P_{\mathrm{quantum}} = \frac{\hbar^2}{2m}(3\pi^2 n)^{2/3}. \end{equation} Together they ensure that \[ P_{\mathrm{Casimir}} + P_{\mathrm{quantum}} > P_{\mathrm{gravity}}, \] achieving a traversable equilibrium well below any collapse threshold.
theory.technical_interpretation: Together They Ensure That is an inequality / bound, written P_Casimir + P_quantum > P_gravity. It states an inequality or bound that constrains the admissible range of the quantity. It is built from P_. Within the Trawin Zero Point registry it serves as one element of the framework's mathematical narrative.

formation.note: Introduced in the derivation where achieving a traversable equilibrium well below any collapse threshold.
